sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Understanding Momentum Shifts in (TR)
feed.summary: This article analyzes Tootsie Roll Industries Inc. (NYSE: TR) using AI models, indicating a strong overweight bias across all horizons. It highlights an exceptional 30.8:1 risk-reward setup, targeting a 9.1% gain with a 0.3% risk, and outlines three institutional trading strategies tailored to various risk profiles. The analysis also includes multi-timeframe signal analysis with strong signals for near-term, mid-term, and long-term horizons, providing specific support and resistance levels.

feed.title: Antibiotic developer Acurx sets March 13 call on 2025 results, business update
feed.summary: Acurx Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ: ACXP) will report its full year and fourth quarter 2025 financial results on Friday, March 13, 2026, at 8:00 a.m. ET, before U.S. markets open. Management, including CEO David P. Luci and CFO Robert G. Shawah, will host a conference call to discuss the results and provide a business update. The company's lead antibiotic candidate, Ibezapolstat, is preparing for international Phase 3 clinical trials for C. difficile Infection (CDI) and has received positive regulatory guidance from both the EMA and FDA.

feed.title: Applied Materials expands Israel R&D hub months after layoffs
feed.summary: Three months after layoffs affecting 100 employees in Israel, Applied Materials has opened a new research and development center in Rehovot. This expansion is part of a "hundreds of millions of dollars" investment in Israel over the past three years. The new facility reinforces Israel's role as a strategic knowledge hub for the company, particularly in process control systems and analytical software for chip manufacturing. Applied Materials plans to recruit 300 additional employees this year, demonstrating continued commitment to its Israeli operations.

feed.title: Vodafone Adds 17.2 Million Shares to Treasury in Ongoing Buyback
feed.summary: Vodafone Group Plc has repurchased 17.15 million of its ordinary shares on February 16, 2026, as part of an ongoing buyback program. These shares, acquired at an average price of 113.93 pence, will be held in treasury, increasing the total to 1.63 billion shares and reducing the free float. This move is part of Vodafone's capital management strategy, aiming to support earnings per share and signal confidence in its financial position.

feed.title: Why Vertex Pharmaceuticals (VRTX) Is Up 6.0% After Profit Rebound And Non-CF Pipeline Update - And What's Next
feed.summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals reported strong full-year 2025 results with a revenue increase to US$12.00 billion and a significant rebound to a US$3.95 billion net profit. The company's 2026 guidance anticipates growing contributions from non-cystic fibrosis products like CASGEVY and JOURNAVX, alongside a new collaboration in autoimmune diseases. This shift towards non-CF revenue and improved profitability is reshaping Vertex's investment narrative and risk profile.

feed.title: Coca-Cola Europacific Partners launches €1bn share buyback program
feed.summary: Coca-Cola Europacific Partners (CCEP) announced a new €1 billion share buyback program, set to begin on February 18, 2026, and conclude by the end of February 2027. The program aims to reduce the company's issued share capital, with all repurchased shares to be cancelled. Goldman Sachs will execute the initial tranche of up to €500 million across U.S. and London trading venues.

feed.title: FirstEnergy Transmission and Transource Energy Joint Venture Receive Approval for Major Electric Transmission Project in Central Ohio
feed.summary: FirstEnergy Transmission and Transource Energy, through their joint venture Grid Growth Ventures LLC, have received approval from PJM Interconnection for a significant electric transmission project in central Ohio. This project, which includes over 300 miles of new 765-kilovolt lines and substation upgrades, aims to enhance grid capacity, reliability, and support economic growth in the fast-growing Columbus region. The collaboration leverages the combined expertise of FirstEnergy, American Electric Power (AEP), and Evergy to address rising electricity demand from new manufacturing facilities, data centers, and electric vehicles.
